name: Link, Rose
motto: "Knowledge is power," saith the sage, and if that be true, Miss Link should be a wonder as a teacher.
description: Her discussions of the ways and means used in training the minds of the young will ever be remembered by her fortunate classmates. The history of Education was a series of triumphs, while Moulton Hall was made a place of study by her. In history Miss Cruttenden and Mr. Layton both thank heaven for sending them such a genius with a mind bent on absorbing the wealth of the ages. Here's to your success, Rose, ever onward and upward. From the looks of a certain diamond we may venture to quote: "Rose now, Rose forever, Link now, but not forever."

name: Tidd, James W.
description: It has been a great pleasure to have James with us these last four years. He is one of those congenial fellows who always tries to have the other person enjoy himself. James has made his mark at K. S. N. C. Brilliant in conversation, studious in the classroom, and agile in athletics are only some of his high points. His most frequent words are "Raise you five."

name: Robinson, Henry J.
description: When we think of Mr. Robinson we involuntarily think of the Kentonian, and when we think of either we feel a thrill of pride. An active participant in all student activities, he stands forth as an example worthy of emulation. Henry is a star in indoor baseball as well. Keep it up, Old Top, we expect great things of you. Surely he hath a heart of steel, for has he not run the gauntlet of an overwhelming number of the fair sex and escaped, "The tie that binds?"

name: Hostettler, Clyde O.
description: Is he married? He is. Having answered this most important question we will now proceed with our talk. Hostettler is a student of science, having spent much time while in Kent in the chemical laboratory. His ability is not measured by his knowledge of science, however. He is a good student in all work which he undertakes. Clyde has the necessary confidence and ability to make good. His friends in Kent are sure that he will, too.

name: Keller, Karl N.
motto: "Then here's to Keller, such a cute little feller; He's a friend tried and true to sure. If you have had some bad news, Or perhaps have the blues, Just talk with this chap—he's the cure."
description: Yes! Karl comes from Massillon, to be sure, but please don't associate "Bug Houses" with him, as a more wide-awake little man never trod the halls of K. S. N. C. The Wise Ones have told us that a man can be well judged by the way he spends his leisure hours. Keller devotes his to indoor baseball, and, it is rumored, makes frequent trips to Lowry Hall. He says he is not in love, though, and he hasn't time to get married anyway; but time alone will tell. Good luck, Karl! May the success which you have proved you deserve be yours.

name: McClintock, Kenneth Norton
description: We are indeed glad that Mr. McClintock saw fit to take his post-graduate work at K. S. N. C. His experience gained from residing in one of Ohio's greatest Universities, coupled with his sterling scholarship and his ability to make friends have made him a trusted and valued friend of all who have been fortunate enough to make his acquaintance.

name: Hershner, Cora Vinetta
motto: Work while you work, Play while you play, This is her motto From day unto day.
description: A quiet, unassuming girl with a bright outlook on life. With a smile and a pleasant word for all.

name: Roberts, Gilbert
motto: "Veni, vidi, vici."
description: For the past four years Gilbert has stood like a Saul in the student body of K. S. N. C. At the cost of much personal sacrifice he has freely given his time and energy toward furthering the student welfare and happiness here. Always calm and deliberate, he possesses that rare quality of thinking a subject through logically, considering all possible points of view. Because of his wonderful capacity for knowledge, his broad-mindedness, and altruism, he will some day say: Although hardly a woman hater, he has spent little time this winter entertaining the fair sex in our midst—but who knows?

name: Wheeler, Minnie Ethel
description: Calm, unruffled, dignified, and faithful in performing the smallest details of her work, we find Miss Wheeler intensely interested in Household Science. Is there any special reason, Minnie?

name: Kane, Harry W.
description: Mr. Kane might well be termed "Dean of the Class of 1918" as far as age goes, but in spirit Mr. Kane is as young as the youngest of us. He has the spirit that will never grow old. His broad experience as a teacher and his ability to recognize and relate some of the bright and witty things in life have earned him the place that he holds in the memory of each of his classmates.

name: Pierson, Annette E.
description: And here we see our friend Annette, A place in our hearts she has won, With her smile, and a summer heart, And then loves her some more.

name: Ellsworth, Gertrude C.
description: Straight and fair is our Gertrude, And true to the core; Everyone of us loves her, And then loves her some more.

name: Schlageter, Mildred E.
description: She is happy the livelong day, With her smile she has won us all, The success of this book is due to her work, K. S. N. C. will miss her next fall.

name: Beaney, M. Ruth
description: "Beaney" is very studious, In teaching she excels, We like to listen, To the tales that she tells.

name: Tichy, Libby
description: Our Libby has quite lost her heart, She cannot decide, whether to Martin or Mart, But I am sure you will all agree, It is an art teacher she should be.

name: Sutherland, Mildred
description: She is quite a quiet lassie; Pearl never has much to say, Though she has a smile quite sassy, just why this is we cannot tell; She is like a ray of sun, and very full of fun. But she is busy all the day, playing and working equally well.

name: Merrill, Pearl
description: She is quite a quiet lassie; Pearl never has much to say, Though she has a smile quite sassy, just why this is we cannot tell; She is like a ray of sun, and very full of fun. But she is busy all the day, playing and working equally well.

name: Seeley, Augusta
motto: "A heart that feels and eyes that smile, and the dearest gifts that heaven supplies."
description: Augusta, a sincere maid, who's always thinking about her grade; She is a true friend to all in need, and always ready when it comes to a feed.

name: Schmalzle, Carrie
description: She's busy every moment with some project quite worth while; As a friend she's true and faithful, and she wears a welcome smile.

name: Hoover, Gladys M.
motto: "We're thankful for one who is cheerful, In spite of life's troubles, I say; Who sings of a brighter tomorrow, Because of the clouds of today."

name: Brown, Nina M.
description: A wonderful little girl is Nina, Always so sweet and so loving to meet, With a smile for all and a voice divine, Another like her you never will find.

name: Farrow, Clara J.
description: A "physical ed." girl is she, Just as sweet as she can be. On week days she "loves" to stay in Kent, But on Friday her steps are toward Cleveland bent.

name: Thorpe, Irene A.
description: She's clever and bright, and curious, too, This girl who is called Irene; And a true, true friend she will prove to all Who have won and kept her esteem.

name: Waltenbaugh, Lucy A.
description: Lucy, Lucy, whither away? "I'm going a camping, Sir," she said, "For many a mile away from here, I have a man who's very dear."

name: Conroy, Lillian C.
description: Lillian Conroy is little we know, But precious wee things in small packages grow. Always she works when there's work to be done, Always she helps when she thinks there is fun.

name: Rex, Dorothy
motto: "A form more fair, a face more sweet, Ne'er hath it been my lot to meet."

name: Rice, Dorothy
motto: "Dorothy Rice, Medina, O. And her eyes are dark and humid, Like the depths on depths of lustre."

name: Rex, Florence
motto: "Florence REX, Medina, O. A countenance in which did meet, Sweet records, promises as sweet."

name: Meta F. Chapman
description: Physical Ed. is her special art. As a first class teacher, she's made a good start.

name: H. Grace Luneman
motto: "Come weel, come woe, I care na by, I'll take what heaven will send me, O. Nae ither care in life have I, But live and love my Johnnie, O."

name: Esther E. Alker
description: Among us she has tread her quiet way, For somehow she has never words to say. She looks and looks with wonder in her eyes, And what she cannot see, her wit supplies.

name: Loyd Noble
motto: "Her fingers and eyes, her mouth, her chin, Are full of a turbulent glee; And try as I may, I cannot escape The spell that they throw over me."

name: Nina N. Frye
description: A lovely maid, most dear and taking, When we are sad, she makes us glad, With funny speeches she's always making, It is not so much what she says we measure, But the accent of her voice, we treasure.

name: Wade, Alice
motto: "cheerfulness."
description: Many a scrape has she been in, Of course, all due to her frankness; But a "true blue" girl she'll always be.

name: Pew, Marion
description: She's witty and clever, and happy and bright; To know her, my dear, is indeed a delight. Her friends will regret the day they must part From this little maid who has a place in their heart.

name: Crittenden, Celeste M.
description: You hear Celeste talk about "Hall." Does she mean Moulton? Not at all, But one who admires this fair, sweet girl, And by his attentions keeps her in a whirl.

name: Hilles, Lucille
description: Here's to the lassie, with fond eyes and sassy, Who made such a hit in the Senior play; As a lover, ideal, she surely was real, And acted the part in a heart-stealing way.

name: Powell, Dorothy M.
motto: "Her air, her manners, all who saw admired, Courteous, tho coy, and gentle tho retired, The joy of youth and health are in her eyes displayed, And ease of heart, her every look conveyed."

name: Margery E. Mcdevitt
motto: "Dancing, don't you?"
description: To eat, to sleep, and to dance are three things she loves to do. When asked which she likes the best, she answers, "Dancing, don't you?" Margery, a maiden sweet, to have her with us is a treat. She never shouts, she's always nice, but really, she is afraid of mice.

name: Elsie Schope
description: She is short but sweet, to hear her tell a story is indeed a treat. And will surely win her glory.

name: Genevieve B. Coleman
description: Here's to a girl who is always neat, she goes on her way rejoicing. For here is a girl who'll always smile, no matter how often you meet.

name: Alberta Lynne
description: She goes on her way rejoicing, working like a beaver, and having little to say.

name: Maguire, Beatrice
description: What shall we say of Beatrice Maguire? For it's into their room on a chilly eve, She drops in for a chat.

name: Hudson, Hazel J.
description: A whole-souled winsome lassie! Her heart has room for all, And you'll be sure to share it, If you're within her call.

name: Lippman, Bertha E.
description: Bertha has been with us only one short year; But we have all decided that she surely is a dear.

name: Sames, Wilda M.
motto: "A little maiden of sweet and lovely disposition, Whose charming ways win many hearts; A maiden whose friendship is worth while."

name: Becker, Rachel
description: A friendly lass with big brown eyes Who, work and study, does not despise; And when it comes to a dance or feed, You'll find her right on hand, indeed.

name: Florence, Wilt
description: A sweet and quiet lass, Is quite a favorite with her class. Sweet as a primrose, shy as a deer, She walks among us spreading cheer.

name: Mabel, L. Culp
description: I'm wicked I is, I'm naughty wicked, Anyhow I can't help it.

name: Marion, Pelton
description: This likeness of Marion will be A keepsake for you and for me. Her sweet manner remember, For none would offend her, But always small will she be.

name: Ada, M. Printz
description: Our Ada is so very quiet, She is so very deep, But when it comes to fun and feasts, She's the liveliest in our suite.

name: C. Idetta, Lutz
description: Unassuming and quiet, but so kind to all, Her pleasant, good nature, we'll always recall.

name: Manchester, Ellen
description: Her sunny way and curling hair make her a favorite, I declare.

name: Trescott, Betty
description: She's noted for her solos and her laughing Irish wit, no matter where she sings, she always makes a hit.

name: Hershman, Esther
description: She who works hard the live-long day, never complains or has much to say; to teach, some think, is her only knack, but let's all be sure and just ask "Mack."

name: Richardson, Marie R.
description: Twist "Ed. and Gym." she's quite in trim, for many a fine occupation, "The housewife's the best for today," says "Jim." while school marm's the cry of her wise "Education."

name: Cubbison, Ruth M.
description: She's little but she's sweet, all her friends can tell you this; for there's sunshine in the smile and the eyes of this fair miss.

name: Sherbon, Josie
description: This studious normal student Is busy all the time; One so quiet and so dignified Is very hard to find.

name: Darling, Catherine P.
description: Arguing, tatting and crochet, Catherine did the livelihood day. Then to training school she went, And now she's more on learning bent.

name: Gamble, Martha
description: A maid of intellect is she, As bright and witty as can be. Tho' little she is mighty, As you will all agree.

name: Hess, Ona E.
description: Tho' she is small of stature, We cannot say she is frail, Her face has a winning kindness, As a friend she ne'er will fail.

name: Paxton, Estella R.
description: Surely, we know this maiden, She greets each with a smile, And all the long, long while Her arms with books are laden.

name: Hazel, Montague
description: A ready tongue, a ready wit, Even in Geography she possesses it. She is busy here and busy there Altho she seldom has a care.

name: Kasner, E. Genieve
description: Of all sad words, These words I hate; Get up; get up; It's after eight.

name: Johnson, M. Ruth
motto: "Ray-fringed eyelids of the morn, Roof not a glance, so keen as thine. If aught of prophecy be mine, Thou wilt not live in vain."

name: Schriver, Rachel I.
description: Domestic Science is your realm, Dancing is your art; But by your waffles, Rachel, dear, You'll win some good man's heart.

name: Rowland, Caroline
description: Quiet, conscientious, above all, true blue, You're the lucky one, if she's a friend to you.

name: Joliat, Helen C.
description: We'll miss her smiles when she is gone. We'll miss her dimples, too. It does not seem that she can help But miss her class-mates too.

name: Ginther, Clara E.
motto: "I make it a practice to put all my worries in the bottom of my heart, sit on the lid and smile."

name: Golden, Margaret R.
description: Moulton Hall her station, Lowry Hall her boarding place, And giggling her vocation.

name: Nelson, Elizabeth E.
description: More modest maid has never tread Dear Kent State Normal's halls. A truly student life she led Within its beauteous walls.

name: Gordon, Esther C.
motto:
"With loyal heart and spirit brave,
With a soul that is pure and true;
Give to the world the best you have,
And reward will come back to you."
description: Clara is a friend, tried and true, Whatever she starts of work or play, She always sees it through. We could listen to her every day, As in her unassuming way, She merely touches the piano keys, And you hear most beautiful melodies. Full of frolic, full of fun, Sympathetic with everyone. Our lessons we should always get, But the movies haunt me yet.

name: Kreitler, Eleanor J.
motto:
"With loyal heart and spirit brave,
With a soul that is pure and true;
Give to the world the best you have,
And reward will come back to you."
description: Clara is a friend, tried and true, Whatever she starts of work or play, She always sees it through. We could listen to her every day, As in her unassuming way, She merely touches the piano keys, And you hear most beautiful melodies. Full of frolic, full of fun, Sympathetic with everyone. Our lessons we should always get, But the movies haunt me yet.

name: Snyder, Clara B.
motto:
"With loyal heart and spirit brave,
With a soul that is pure and true;
Give to the world the best you have,
And reward will come back to you."
description: Clara is a friend, tried and true, Whatever she starts of work or play, She always sees it through. We could listen to her every day, As in her unassuming way, She merely touches the piano keys, And you hear most beautiful melodies. Full of frolic, full of fun, Sympathetic with everyone. Our lessons we should always get, But the movies haunt me yet.

name: Shiele, Nellie T.
motto:
"With loyal heart and spirit brave,
With a soul that is pure and true;
Give to the world the best you have,
And reward will come back to you."
description: Clara is a friend, tried and true, Whatever she starts of work or play, She always sees it through. We could listen to her every day, As in her unassuming way, She merely touches the piano keys, And you hear most beautiful melodies. Full of frolic, full of fun, Sympathetic with everyone. Our lessons we should always get, But the movies haunt me yet.

name: Hopusch, Myrtle V.
motto:
"With loyal heart and spirit brave,
With a soul that is pure and true;
Give to the world the best you have,
And reward will come back to you."
description: Clara is a friend, tried and true, Whatever she starts of work or play, She always sees it through. We could listen to her every day, As in her unassuming way, She merely touches the piano keys, And you hear most beautiful melodies. Full of frolic, full of fun, Sympathetic with everyone. Our lessons we should always get, But the movies haunt me yet.

name: Fern, I. Gamberling
motto: "To see her is to love her, And love but her forever; For nature made her what she is, And never made another."

name: M. Agnes, Huston
description: She is the most winsome of lasses. In all things she surpasses, Those who won't work, and those who will shirk, But she likes candy made of molasses.

name: Veronica, Paul
motto: "She is a friend indeed, With all a friend's virtues, shining bright; It was no broken reed You leaned on, when you trusted in her might."

name: Ruth, M. Gawn
description: Little, but Oh my!

name: Dorothy, G. Henneman
description: O what shall we say of this quiet little miss? For hardly a sound do we hear; But she smiles all day in the happiest way, The hearts of her friends to cheer.

name: Marsh, Dorothy
motto: "Ever in cheerfulness mood art thou, when others are filled with gloomy forebodings of ill, and see only ruin before them, Happy art thou, as if every day thou hadst picked up a horseshoe."

name: Voit, Olive F.
motto: "On the first floor there lived a maid, She was known to her friends as Pete, We'll all miss her smile and her friendly ways, When no longer we chance to meet."

name: McIntosh, Cleo M.
motto: "Clever and quiet, with little to say, Works quite hard the livelong day, Always a smile in her own sweet way, This is the way she passes the day."

name: Orr, Bessie M.
motto: "If I were but a painter, I would paint her here for you; For in our halls, a finer girl Than she, we never knew."

name: Penty, Elsie M.
motto: "There was a little girl Her name was Elsie Penty. She came to Kent State Normal, And of knowledge she got a plenty."

name: Schlessman, Anna E.
description: Like the wings of night—is her coal-black hair; Like the sparkling water, her eyes; But her book is always before her face, And deep are her thoughts, we surmise.

name: White, Mary
description: Here is to Mary, the girl of all girls, The purest, the best, most precious of pearls. Mist of dew gleams in her big eyes of blue, You'd better be careful, she'll play jokes on you.

name: Hoover, Olive M.
description: She is not always as sober as this, But she is modest, sweet and true, If you give her a smile she'll never fail, To return a smile to you.

name: Messenger, Ellen R.
description: When in need of "pop" call on Ellen, For "Pep" is her middle name, With a laugh she is always ready, And above all, she is game.

name: Frazier, Theresa
description: A smile from everyone, and a word of cheer; She certainly has made a host of friends this year.

name: Noble, Marion L.
description: Marion has a noble face. She works quite hard at home to make, She says it's for Domestic Art, Me-thinks it's for her own sweetheart.

name: Wise, Mildred M.
description: Wise in word and Wise in name, Wise in all, she'll win her fame. Just one thing I can't see at all, Why one so wise should be so small.

name: Martens, Anne J.
motto: "Sweet the strain When the song, the singer has been lost."

name: Chase, Alta M.
description: She is one in K. S. N. C. Who helps us as the need might be. She studies hard from morn 'til night, And wasteth much of the midnight light.

name: Garmon, Charlotte M.
description: She is often gay and happy, But can also serious be; Always glad to be of service, She just suits you to a T.

name: Gawn, Eva M.
motto: "An inborn grace that nothing lacked Of culture or appliance; The warmth of genial courtesy, The calm of self-reliance."

name: Wooley, Edna M.
description: To know her is to love her, To love her is to woo, O! golden headed damsel, What can the poor men do?

name: Laber, Esther C.
description: We all do know this maiden, She is quiet, yet happy too, Her shining eyes and dimpling cheek, Do oft some mischief brew.

name: Ferguson, Mary E.
description: Mary, Mary, not a bit contrary, How does your "garten" grow? With Billy Lane and sister Jane, And twelve more kiddies all in a row.

name: McNeil, Grace V.
description: Grace is quiet, unassuming and composed. Always sweet as a wild briar rose. So kind and so true. She is one among few.

name: Bowden, Louise
description: Knocking at the doors, Crying through the locks, "Are you 'wee ones' in your beds? For it's half past ten o'clock."

name: Wilkinson, Nell M.
description: Nellie, our queen of knowledge now, Will be a Psychologist, I trow. Her aim to win is great, But loyalty to friends has weight. Her room is open all day long, To many a happy and loving throng.

name: Lang, Alma
motto: "Knowledge is power,"
description: So said the seer, And knowledge to Mrs. Lang Is very dear.

name: Beal, Florence
description: They say that all good things on earth Come down in parcels small. Then think, my friends, how she must be Valued above us all.

name: Davis, Esther R.
description: Who can forget her quiet ways, Who can forget her smiles, Who can forget her friendship deep, Though between us lie many miles?

name: Hanson, Jennie M.
motto: "Where there's a will, there's a way,"
description: At least that's what Jennie would say, When she knows she is right— She sticks to it tight— It's useless to try her to sway.

name: Griggy, Madeline R.
description: I'm awfully fond of "little" boys, O, goodness, aren't they cute? My life with troubles is surely awhirl, Twixt Si and Ed, and boys and a girl.
